metadata.title: EMA Guideline on the development of new medicinal products for the treatment of Ulcerative Colitis (Revision 1)
metadata.note: Replaces CHMP/EWP/18463/2006. Adopted by CHMP 28 June 2018, PDCO 29 June 2018, in effect 1 January 2019. Covers adult and paediatric UC including pouchitis, acute severe colitis, and special populations.

rules.rule_text: The diagnosis of UC must be based on three independent elements: (1) patient symptoms (diarrhoea, rectal discharge of blood and/or pus); (2) endoscopic findings (decreased/absence of vascular pattern, erythema, friability, granularity, ulcerations in colorectal mucosa); and (3) histological findings (crypt distortion/abscess, neutrophils, ulceration). Infectious causes of colitis and malignancy must be ruled out before enrolment.
rules.verbatim_quote: The diagnosis of UC should be based on patient symptoms (diarrhoea and rectal discharge of blood and/or pus), endoscopic findings... and histological findings... Infectious causes of colitis and malignancy must be ruled out.

rules.rule_text: The exact definition of 'adequate course of treatment' (dosage and duration) for establishing refractoriness must be pre-specified prior to patient inclusion, clearly defined, and adequately justified. For corticosteroids, ECCO definitions of refractoriness are considered acceptable. For biologics, primary non-response definitions must be similarly pre-specified.
rules.verbatim_quote: The exact definition of 'adequate course of treatment' (i.e. dosage and duration of treatment) must be pre-specified (prior to inclusion), clearly defined and adequately justified.
exceptions: ECCO definitions acceptable for corticosteroid refractoriness

rules.rule_text: UC in remission must be defined as mucosal healing (MH) — defined as absence of macroscopic signs of active inflammation as judged by endoscopy — combined with no or very mild symptoms. The precise definition depends on the validated instruments used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with mucosal healing (MH) (for the purpose of this guideline MH is defined as absence of macroscopic signs of active inflammation as judged by endoscopy) who have no or very mild symptoms and signs are considered in remission.

rules.rule_text: The treatment goal for UC clinical trials is achieving and maintaining both symptomatic AND endoscopic remission. Demonstrating effect on the inflammatory process is essential — lack of control of inflammation even with symptom control is correlated with poor long-term outcome.
rules.verbatim_quote: The goal of treatment of ulcerative colitis is achieving and maintaining symptomatic and endoscopic remission.

rules.rule_text: Symptomatic relief must be evaluated using patient-reported outcomes (PROs). An instrument used as a primary outcome measure in pivotal UC trials must be rigorously validated; it must include clinically important symptoms such as stool frequency and rectal discharge of blood. The symptom part of the Mayo score may be used until fully validated PRO instruments are available.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2 — Methods to assess efficacy criteria
rules.verbatim_quote: An instrument to be used as primary outcome measure in pivotal clinical trials in UC should be rigorously validated... the symptom part of the Mayo score may be used until fully validated scores are available.
exceptions: Mayo symptom subscale acceptable as interim measure pending full PRO validation

rules.rule_text: The endoscopic effect on the inflammatory process must be evaluated directly by endoscopy using validated indices such as UCEIS (UC endoscopic index of severity) or the endoscopic part of the Mayo score. Surrogate markers of inflammation such as faecal calprotectin are supportive but cannot replace direct endoscopic evaluation as a primary endpoint. The total Mayo score including physician's global assessment is NOT of primary interest.
rules.source_section: Section 5.2 — Methods to assess efficacy criteria
rules.verbatim_quote: The effect on the inflammatory process as such should be evaluated directly by endoscopy... Surrogate markers of inflammation, such as faecal calprotectin are considered supportive but cannot replace direct endoscopic evaluation of inflammation... the total Mayo score including physician's global assessment is not of primary interest.

rules.rule_text: Estimands must be pre-specified in accordance with ICH E9(R1). For maintenance/long-term trials, treatment discontinuation must be handled using a composite strategy (counted as treatment failure). Initiation of corticosteroid rescue medication — whether foreseen or not in the protocol — must be counted as treatment failure in the primary estimand. Failure to taper steroids per the protocol-fixed schedule is a treatment failure unless the deviation is minor and pre-defined as acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: The initiation of corticosteroid treatment irrespective of such being foreseen or not in the trial protocol should also be considered as a failure of the study drug (composite strategy).
exceptions: Minor deviations from steroid taper schedule may not need to be considered treatment failure if pre-specified and justified

rules.rule_text: Co-primary endpoints must be: (1) the proportion of patients with symptomatic remission; and (2) the proportion of patients with endoscopic remission. Both endpoints must be met for the trial to be considered successful.
rules.verbatim_quote: Consequently, co-primary endpoints of treatment should concern: (1) The proportion of patients with symptomatic remission, and (2) The proportion of patients with endoscopic remission.

rules.rule_text: Endoscopic remission must be defined and justified based on the instrument used. When using the Mayo endoscopic subscore, a score of 0 or 1 may define endoscopic healing; the more stringent definition (score 0) is preferred. Endoscopic adjudication must be performed, preferably by central reading; if decentralised, standardisation of reading must be demonstrated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Adjudication of endoscopic evidence of activity should be performed, preferably by central reading of the examinations. If decentralised reading of examination is performed, standardization of reading should be demonstrated.
exceptions: Less stringent Mayo subscore 1 acceptable based on patient severity or early time-point assessment

rules.rule_text: Irrespective of the scale and thresholds used for symptomatic remission, the definition of remission must encompass cessation of rectal bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: Irrespective of scale and thresholds used, the definition of remission should encompass cessation of rectal bleeding.

rules.rule_text: Mandatory secondary endpoints must include: combined MH and symptomatic remission; symptomatic and endoscopic response; steroid-free remission (in induction studies where steroids are not tapered); histological evaluation including number of patients achieving histological normalisation; combined endoscopic MH plus faecal calprotectin normalisation plus histological normalisation; laboratory measures of inflammation; time to remission and time to response.
rules.verbatim_quote: The following secondary endpoints should be reported: Patients achieving both MH and symptomatic remission; Patients achieving response... Histological evaluation of mucosal inflammation, including number of patients achieving histological normalisation...

rules.rule_text: Study enrolment requires recent (within 1 month) endoscopic and histological documentation of mucosal inflammation. A Mayo score of 9–12 may be used as an inclusion criterion, but patients must also have a minimum level of mucosal inflammation (e.g. endoscopic Mayo subscore ≥2). Minimum disease duration must be at least 3 months; shorter duration requires justification.
rules.verbatim_quote: Degree and extent of mucosal inflammation should be documented by recent visualisation of the gastrointestinal tract, by recent (1 month) endoscopic and histological examination... The minimum time from onset of symptoms should be at least 3 months at inclusion.
exceptions: Shorter disease duration justifiable if care is taken to exclude diarrhoea due to infections

rules.rule_text: At least two confirmatory trials are required to support a general 'treatment of ulcerative colitis' indication. Trials must be randomised, double-blind, and include parallel active comparator and/or placebo arms. Both short-term (6–12 weeks) and long-term (≥12 months total) efficacy must be demonstrated; these are considered co-primary and no multiplicity adjustment is needed.
rules.source_section: Section 6.4.2.1 — Studies for a general claim
rules.verbatim_quote: To fulfil a claim for the treatment of ulcerative colitis, it is expected that at least two confirmatory trials are provided... For a general claim 'treatment of UC', short as well as long-term efficacy should be demonstrated. Thus, assessment of short- and long-term efficacy are considered co-primary and no adjustment for multiplicity is necessary.

rules.rule_text: Total study duration including the short-term induction phase must be at least 12 months. Treat-through designs or re-randomisation of responders to placebo or test drug are both acceptable. Placebo-controlled study duration must not exceed 6 months; adequate escape procedures must be pre-defined.
rules.verbatim_quote: In both instances, the total duration (including the short-term phase) should be at least 12 months... it may be advisable to restrict the use of placebo control for a maximum of 6 months (with adequate escape procedures at intermediate time-points).

rules.rule_text: For a 'maintenance of remission' claim, the trial must demonstrate that patients in complete remission at study entry remain in remission throughout a full 52-week study period.
rules.verbatim_quote: For a claim of 'maintenance of remission' it needs to be demonstrated that patients being in complete remission at study entry remain in remission throughout a full 52-week study period.

rules.rule_text: For a first-line indication, a direct comparison with current generally accepted standard first-line treatment is required. For a second-line indication (after failure/intolerance to primary therapy), placebo is an acceptable comparator; however, an active comparator may also be required depending on the exact target population. For add-on designs, established therapy must be continued as background therapy in both arms.
rules.verbatim_quote: In order to support a first line indication... it is necessary to provide a direct comparison with current generally accepted standard first line treatment. For a second line indication (after failure or intolerance to primary therapy), placebo is an acceptable comparator.

rules.rule_text: Concomitant 5-ASA must be at a stable dose for >2 weeks before inclusion in induction studies and >4-6 weeks for maintenance studies. AZA/MP must be at stable doses for at least 3 months before inclusion. Patients entering the study on corticosteroids must have steroids tapered per a fixed, standardised schedule; tapering must occur within 12 weeks of entering the maintenance phase at a rate equivalent to 2.5–5 mg prednisolone per week.
rules.verbatim_quote: For 5-ASA, a stable dose for > 2 weeks is appropriate for induction studies and > 4-6 weeks for maintenance studies. Treatment with AZA/MP requires stable doses for at least 3 months... Tapering schedules should be standardised. Usually tapering can be done at a rate equivalent with 2.5 to 5 mg of prednisolone/week.

rules.rule_text: Statistical analysis plans must be aligned with the pre-specified estimands (ICH E9(R1)). Missing data arising from patient refusal to undergo repeat endoscopy is not an intercurrent event and must be handled via imputation (e.g. multiple imputation based on remission probability of patients still on treatment) in line with the estimand. Statistical analysis choices must be pre-specified and fully justified in the study protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Choices made regarding statistical analysis, including the handling of missing data, must be aligned with the agreed target(s) of estimation... refusal to undergo repeated endoscopy... is not an intercurrent event and missing data for these patients needs to be handled in the statistical analysis, in accordance with the target of estimation.

rules.rule_text: For immunomodulatory products, safety characterisation must include: serious infections, autoimmune diseases, and tumour facilitating/inducing potential. Special attention is warranted for women of childbearing potential. Long-term immunogenicity (binding and neutralising antibodies) and re-treatment outcomes must be investigated; re-treatment data after a longer drug-free interval must be collected as post-marketing commitments.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be given to the possibility of occurrence of serious infections, autoimmune diseases and the tumour facilitating/inducing potential of these products. As UC affects young women of childbearing potential, special attention is warranted in this population.

rules.rule_text: The paediatric clinical development programme must include patients from 2 years of age unless significant safety signals preclude it. Population PK modelling is preferred over separate PK studies. Endoscopic evaluation is mandatory; symptomatic response alone is not acceptable in paediatric trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical development program should include children from 2 years of age and older unless there are significant safety concerns... population PK modelling on the basis of all available data... Symptomatic response alone is not considered acceptable in children.

rules.rule_text: In paediatric UC trials, endoscopic mucosal healing must be assessed using the Mayo score (score of 0 or ≤1). The PUCAI score is acceptable as a surrogate for symptomatic remission (defined as PUCAI <10 points) in the absence of a fully validated paediatric PRO. The primary endpoint for paediatric maintenance trials must be sustained relapse-free corticosteroid-free remission (maintaining both symptomatic remission and endoscopic MH).
rules.verbatim_quote: Remission should be defined as symptomatic remission accompanied by endoscopic MH... Endoscopic MH should be assessed by the Mayo score (score of 0, or ≤1)... the use of the PUCAI as a surrogate for symptomatic remission is considered acceptable. Symptomatic remission can therefore be defined as PUCAI<10 points. The primary endpoint of maintenance trials should be sustained relapse-free corticosteroid-free remission.

rules.rule_text: For paediatric safety, post-authorisation long-term observational studies (registries) must be used to collect safety data on maturation and development. The registry must preferably be disease-based (rather than product-based) to collect data from sufficient numbers of patients treated with different medicinal products. For younger children (<6 years), measures to evaluate the impact of the experimental therapy on vaccination response must be implemented.
rules.verbatim_quote: The long-term evaluation of safety requires collection of data from larger number of patients for a longer period of time, potentially into adulthood... The registry should preferably be an established disease-based (rather than product-based) clinical registry.

rules.rule_text: Studies in acute severe colitis must use the Truelove & Witts definition. For steroid-refractory acute severe UC, cyclosporine or infliximab must be used as the active comparator. Avoidance of colectomy (short- and long-term) is a relevant primary endpoint in this population. Daily evaluations are required.
rules.source_section: Section 7.3.2 — Patients with acute severe colitis
rules.verbatim_quote: The definition of acute severe colitis, which has most commonly been used, is that of Truelove & Witts... Studies in steroid refractory acute, severe ulcerative colitis should be active controlled (cyclosporine or infliximab). Avoidance of colectomy short- and long-term are relevant primary endpoints in this population.
exceptions: For non-steroid-refractory patients, steroids are the comparator of choice

rules.rule_text: Studies in pouchitis must be double-blind, randomised, and controlled. For acute pouchitis (<4 weeks), metronidazole or ciprofloxacin are the comparators. For chronic antibiotic-resistant pouchitis, placebo control is acceptable. Efficacy must demonstrate co-primary endpoints: both symptomatic remission and mucosal healing (including histological assessment). The PDAI (18-point Pouchitis Disease Activity Index) may be used provided response and remission are convincingly defined with clinically relevant effects in all components.
rules.verbatim_quote: Design should be double blind, randomised and controlled... Efficacy in terms of symptoms as well as MH (including histological assessment) (co-primary endpoints) should be demonstrated. The 18-point Pouchitis Disease Activity Index (PDAI)... use of PDAI may be acceptable provided that response and remission are convincingly defined.

rules.rule_text: Adequate numbers of elderly patients must be included in UC clinical trials. Clinical effects in elderly patients may be influenced by reduced glomerular filtration rates, increased susceptibility to adverse events (e.g. delirium, fractures), and drug-drug interactions due to polypharmacy. These factors must be specifically addressed.
rules.verbatim_quote: It should be ensured that adequate number of elderly patients are included in clinical trials, since clinical effects in these patients may be influenced by factors such as reduced glomerular filtration rates, increased susceptibility to adverse events (e.g. delirium, fractures), and drug-drug interactions in case of polypharmacy.

rules.rule_text: A Risk Management Plan (RMP) is mandatory for immunosuppressive and/or immunomodulating drugs including new biologics. The RMP must monitor infectious and/or malignant complications, adverse reactions across sub-populations, and whether treatment results in a reduction of surgical intervention (colectomy) long-term.
rules.verbatim_quote: A risk management plan will normally have to be implemented in order to monitor possible long-term consequences of use of immunosuppressive and/or immunomodulating drugs, including new biologicals. Particular attention should be paid to infectious and/or malignant complications. Furthermore, adverse reactions in different sub-population should be monitored. Whether new treatments result in reduction in surgical intervention long-term is also of interest.
